hero.question: Will Illinois win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 17.8%, the crowd is pricing Illinois as a true long shot with roughly 1-in-6 odds to hoist the trophy, which feels about right for a program that finished outside the tournament last year and will need everything to break perfectly in 2026. The real tell is whether Terrence Shannon Jr. stays healthy and the roster doesn't get picked apart by the draft, but $1.5M in volume suggests sharp money has already priced in that uncertainty correctly.

daily_take.headline: Illinois at 17.8% is a steal if you believe in Ayo Dosunmu's recruiting class.
daily_take.deck: Illinois futures dropped 0.2 points overnight despite no new injury news or roster moves, suggesting market indifference rather than fundamental deterioration. The market is pricing in last season's tournament disappointment while ignoring that Brad Underwood landed one of the nation's top recruiting classes for 2025-26. At 17.8%, you're getting a Final Four contender at mid-major odds.
